text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any developer. The main goal is to make your code readable and easy for others to understand. One key practice is using clear naming conventions for variables and functions. Instead of naming something "x" or "doStuff," choose names that describe what they represent or do, like "userEmail" or "calculateTotal." It is also important to break your code into small, focused functions that each handle a single task. This approach improves maintainability because it makes testing and updating the code much simpler. When you write clean code, you save time for both yourself and your teammates in the long run.
